window_summary: This window tracks the evolution of PhilJay/MPAndroidChart from early v1.7.x fixes through the v2.0.x major rewrite, v2.1.x feature expansions, v2.2.x enhancements, and the v3.x breaking changes including the elimination of the x-index concept and bumping minSdkVersion to 14.

chunks.title: Version 2.0.0 beta
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: PhilJay/MPAndroidChart
Release: Version 2.0.0 beta
Tag: v2.0.0; draft: False; prerelease: True; published_at: 2015-02-21T14:09:53Z; created_at: 2015-02-21T14:09:36Z
Release notes:
What's new:
- Dual `YAxis` (you can now choose which axis you want your `DataSet` to plot against, either LEFT or RIGHT)
- `CombinedChart` (bar-, line-, scatter- and candle-data can now be displayed together in one chart)
- Removed nineoldandroids-library
- and many more...
Not working yet:
- `HorizontalBarChart`
- Grouped `BarChart`  highlighting
- `CombinedChart` highlighting
- lot's of other stuff :-)
The ReadMe.md documentation is currently outdated. Please refer to the example-project.
Please note that this is only a pre-release and absolutely not bug free and feature complete.
Let me know what you think and inform me about bugs / issues.
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the PhilJay/MPAndroidChart release v2.0.0 beta, the release note introduces dual YAxis, a CombinedChart for displaying bar, line, scatter, and candle data together, removes the nineoldandroids library, and notes that HorizontalBarChart and grouped BarChart highlighting are not yet working.

chunks.title: Bugfixes, new features
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: PhilJay/MPAndroidChart
Release: Bugfixes, new features
Tag: v2.2.5; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2016-05-27T20:35:27Z; created_at: 2016-05-27T15:47:13Z
Release notes:
- New: Horizontal cubic bezier lines for Line chart (through `mode` property)
- New: Allow transparent color in circle hole color (Line chart)
- New: `circleHoleRadius` property for more control over line chart circles
- New: `PieChart` values outside of chart (connected with lines)
- New: `highlightFullBar` for highlighting all values on an x-index at once
- New: Allow setting `xVals` on existing Data objects
- New: Allow bubble sizes to not be normalized against the dataset max
- New: Added _material_ color template
- New: Flag to control whether we draw a limit line's labels
- New: Exploded the Legend-Position enum to support more combinations
- New: Allow drawing borders for bars in a Bar chart
- Improved: Highlighting when multiple values on x-index present (bubble chart, combined chart)
- Fixed: We are now taking into account the `extra` offsets for Pie/Radar chart view
- Fixes regarding MarkerView
- Improvements to Demo, now keeping dataset styling when changin dataset size
- General bugfixes
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the PhilJay/MPAndroidChart release v2.2.5, the release note adds horizontal cubic bezier lines for LineChart, transparent color in circle hole color, circleHoleRadius property, PieChart values outside of chart connected with lines, highlightFullBar for highlighting all values on an x-index, setting xVals on existing Data objects, non-normalized bubble sizes, a material color template, a flag to control drawing limit line labels, exploded Legend-Position enum, borders for bars in BarChart, improved highlighting for multiple values on x-index, fixes for extra offsets in Pie/Radar chart view, MarkerView fixes, improvements to Demo dataset styling, and general bugfixes.

chunks.title: New MPAndroidChart v3.0b
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: PhilJay/MPAndroidChart
Release: New MPAndroidChart v3.0b
Tag: v3.0.0-beta; draft: False; prerelease: True; published_at: 2016-07-01T16:55:50Z; created_at: 2016-07-01T16:54:59Z
Release notes:
- Elimination of the "x-index" concept (each `Entry` now has a `float` x- and y-coordinate)
- Change of the `Entry` constructor from `Entry(float y, int xIndex)` to `Entry(float x, float y)`
- `XAxis` and `YAxis` concept is now similar (`XAxis` no longer represented by `Strings`)
- Separation of the [Realm.io](http://realm.io) support to a [separate repository](https://github.com/PhilJay/MPAndroidChart-Realm)
- Bugfixes and stability improvement
- Many more...
Currently, the [documentation](https://github.com/PhilJay/MPAndroidChart/wiki) is **not up to date** and still refers to versions prior to 3.0 - please have a look a the [example project](https://github.com/PhilJay/MPAndroidChart/tree/master/MPChartExample) to figure out how the new API works. An update of the docs will follow soon.
Please also note that this is a "beta" release that might contain bugs that require fixing. A stable release 3.0 will be out within the next few weeks. If you are certain that you discovered a new issue with this new version, please let me know.
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the PhilJay/MPAndroidChart release v3.0.0-beta, the release note eliminates the x-index concept so each Entry now has float x and y coordinates, changes the Entry constructor, makes XAxis and YAxis concepts similar, separates Realm.io support to a separate repository, and includes bugfixes and stability improvements.

chunks.title: New major release
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: PhilJay/MPAndroidChart
Release: New major release
Tag: v3.0.0; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2016-10-19T15:44:40Z; created_at: 2016-10-19T07:08:42Z
Release notes:
This is a major release, with a few breaking changes. So please pay attention.
Updates:
- The Integer `x-index` is dead. Intoducing: x-values (`float`). Each entry now has an `x` and a `y`.
- Pie/radar charts do not have a concept of X index anymore
- Unified XAxis/YAxis features and experience.
- Unified formatter interfaces for axes.
- Major refactoring of the Highlight mechanism.
- Refactored marker mechanism
- Refactored legend entry mechanism
- Each dataset can now set its own legend form/size/etc.
- More demos
- Many minor bug fixes and improvements
Breaking changes / how to migrate:
- All dataset constructors have changed - they do not take an array of x-indices anymore.
- All entry constructors have changed - they take in an X and a Y.
- Pie and Radar chart entries now have their own classes. So exchange the Entry classes with`PieEntry`/`RadarEntry`
- If you have any old `startAtZeroEnabled` calls - these have been deprecated for a long time. Replace them with `axisMinimum`.
- In general, all functions that accepted x-indices before, now accept an x-value, so you might have to adjust the names a little bit.
- Where `OnChartValueSelectedListener` is implemented, update the `onValueSelected` interface
- Replace `drawSliceTextEnabled` with `drawEntryLabelsEnabled`
- If you have a custom scatter shape - implement an `IShapeRenderer`, and put your rendering code in there
- If you have a custom marker - consider subclassing `MarkerView`/`MarkerImage` or implementing `IMarker`. There are more options than before now...
- If you have used `barSpace` before, move to using `barWidth`
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the PhilJay/MPAndroidChart release v3.0.0, the release note is a major release that eliminates the integer x-index, introduces float x-values, unifies XAxis and YAxis features, refactors highlighting, marker, and legend entry mechanisms, allows each dataset to set its own legend form, adds more demos, and includes many minor bug fixes and improvements.

chunks.title: Version 3.1.0 pre-release
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: PhilJay/MPAndroidChart
Release: Version 3.1.0 pre-release
Tag: v3.1.0-alpha; draft: False; prerelease: True; published_at: 2018-10-29T19:11:17Z; created_at: 2018-10-28T03:56:40Z
Release notes:
### Pre-Release Disclaimer
This is a pre-release, which means it's not fully tested. However, many bugs have still been fixed and I highly recommend that you update anyway to get those latest changes. Please open an issue if you are still running into issues with this release so they can be patched up for the final 3.1 release!
### About
This mostly includes bug fixes, but with a few notable differences. The biggest change is bumping MPAndroidChart's `minSdkVersion` to `14`. If you have a lower version, you should stop supporting it and go up to `14` as well.
Please see the [current distribution usages](https://developer.android.com/about/dashboards/), as less than 1% of Android devices are running versions 15 and lower now.
### Breaking Changes
- `minSdkVersion` has been bumped to **`14`**, read the above.
- `Easing` class has been simplified, old methods of getting easing functions are **DEPRECATED** (35c9fc937ecda0dd2eb1a7b879aa869a48bfad3d)
- `Chart.saveToGallery(name, quality)` now saves as a PNG instead of JPG ([`d67ea48`](https://github.com/PhilJay/MPAndroidChart/commit/d67ea481af1e8528c617dfd404d0c7827b0134b2#diff-fe896948b886c3a2b9093db178e15ac8))
### Additions
- Upgraded project to `androidX`, maven upgraded to `2.1`, gradle upgraded to `4.6`, android gradle to `3.2.1`, android build tools to `28.0.3`
- Example project has been refreshed (d67ea481af1e8528c617dfd404d0c7827b0134b2, 5030b36c86cfa2d076bb6b86fa8fcb37ce58557f)
- Grid lines can be drawn behind or infront of data (c97b8d531d5584b767d0587805c1c95018cf92d6)
- Can use pie slice color for value line color (1e6e58d2c026be332d6756b04d85b33cd3d36c2b)
- BarChart supports gradient colored bars (e4ba3cdc20555b5f7cd793e614ee13406a7605ec)
### Fixes
- Zoom animations fixed (6c54f0b0a91ca87fe39dd63d9095e4f55d6c59ff)
- Many rendering bugs fixed (43ae497ecf7427587687945496c6a70e65f01fcb, aee6058dbb74f4eb1aae8e88c7562d28a91e1e1c, 92c14db5b4de4efd43c9fd74361886b92111ca9f, 89436221db3d83991f9ecd830f871d4ce7da47a4)
- Copying data sets fixed (7abc9cd6696f78c77068ffc278793594b33497fe)
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the PhilJay/MPAndroidChart release v3.1.0-alpha, the release note is a pre-release that bumps minSdkVersion to 14, simplifies the Easing class, changes saveToGallery to save as PNG, upgrades to androidX, refreshes the example project, allows grid lines to be drawn behind or in front of data, enables using pie slice color for value line color, supports gradient colored bars in BarChart, fixes zoom animations, many rendering bugs, and copying data sets.

chunks.title: Version 3.1.0
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: PhilJay/MPAndroidChart
Release: Version 3.1.0
Tag: v3.1.0; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2019-03-20T16:54:58Z; created_at: 2019-03-16T10:53:42Z
Release notes:
### About
This mostly includes bug fixes, but with a few notable differences. The biggest change is bumping MPAndroidChart's `minSdkVersion` to `14`. If you have a lower version, you should stop supporting it and go up to `14` as well.
Please see the [current distribution usages](https://developer.android.com/about/dashboards/), as less than 1% of Android devices are running versions 15 and lower now.
### Breaking Changes
- `minSdkVersion` has been bumped to **`14`**, read the above.
- `Easing` class has been simplified, old methods of getting easing functions are **DEPRECATED** (35c9fc937ecda0dd2eb1a7b879aa869a48bfad3d)
- `Chart.saveToGallery(name, quality)` now saves as a PNG instead of JPG ([`d67ea48`](https://github.com/PhilJay/MPAndroidChart/commit/d67ea481af1e8528c617dfd404d0c7827b0134b2#diff-fe896948b886c3a2b9093db178e15ac8))
### Additions
- Upgraded project to `androidX`, maven upgraded to `2.1`, gradle upgraded to `4.6`, android gradle to `3.2.1`, android build tools to `28.0.3`
- Example project has been refreshed (d67ea481af1e8528c617dfd404d0c7827b0134b2, 5030b36c86cfa2d076bb6b86fa8fcb37ce58557f)
- Grid lines can be drawn behind or infront of data (c97b8d531d5584b767d0587805c1c95018cf92d6)
- Can use pie slice color for value line color (1e6e58d2c026be332d6756b04d85b33cd3d36c2b)
- BarChart supports gradient colored bars (e4ba3cdc20555b5f7cd793e614ee13406a7605ec)
### Fixes
- Zoom animations fixed (6c54f0b0a91ca87fe39dd63d9095e4f55d6c59ff)
- Many rendering bugs fixed (43ae497ecf7427587687945496c6a70e65f01fcb, aee6058dbb74f4eb1aae8e88c7562d28a91e1e1c, 92c14db5b4de4efd43c9fd74361886b92111ca9f, 89436221db3d83991f9ecd830f871d4ce7da47a4)
- Copying data sets fixed (7abc9cd6696f78c77068ffc278793594b33497fe)
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the PhilJay/MPAndroidChart release v3.1.0, the release note bumps minSdkVersion to 14, simplifies the Easing class, changes saveToGallery to save as PNG, upgrades to androidX, refreshes the example project, allows grid lines to be drawn behind or in front of data, enables using pie slice color for value line color, supports gradient colored bars in BarChart, fixes zoom animations, many rendering bugs, and copying data sets.

questions.query_text: In the PhilJay/MPAndroidChart releases, which release introduces a FillFormatter interface for custom line-filling in LineChart and fixes a crash in XLabels?

answer_support.answer_text: provides a FillFormatter interface for custom line-filling in LineChart and fixes a crash in XLabels

questions.query_text: Across the PhilJay/MPAndroidChart releases, how does the chart type support in v2.0.0 beta differ from that in v2.0.1?

options.text: v2.0.0 beta introduces CombinedChart but notes HorizontalBarChart is not working yet, while v2.0.1 adds HorizontalBarChart as a working feature.
v2.0.0 beta removes HorizontalBarChart, while v2.0.1 reintroduces it with performance improvements.
Both releases have identical chart type support, only differing in bugfixes.
v2.0.0 beta includes BubbleChart, while v2.0.1 replaces it with CombinedChart.

questions.expected_answer: v2.0.0 beta introduces CombinedChart but notes HorizontalBarChart is not working yet, while v2.0.1 adds HorizontalBarChart as a working feature.

answer_support.answer_text: introduces dual YAxis, a CombinedChart ... and notes that HorizontalBarChart ... are not yet working

questions.query_text: In the PhilJay/MPAndroidChart release window, order the following release-content states from earliest to latest: (1) a release that adds a callback for fling gestures and new LegendPositions, (2) a release that introduces a BubbleChart chart type, (3) a release that adds support for stepped LineChart and simplifies setting axis min and max value, (4) a release that eliminates the x-index concept and changes the Entry constructor.

answer_support.answer_text: adds new LegendPositions NONE and RIGHT_OF_CHART_INSIDE, a callback for fling gestures
introduces a new BubbleChart chart type
simplifies setting axis min and max value ... adds support for stepped LineChart
eliminates the x-index concept ... changes the Entry constructor
